faq.q: What's the typical tire pressure for my car?
faq.a: Most cars use 30-35 PSI (2.0-2.4 bar). Check your vehicle's door jamb or owner's manual for exact specifications.

faq.q: How does cold weather affect tire pressure?
faq.a: Tire pressure drops in cold conditions. Always measure in the morning when tires are cool, before driving.

faq.q: What happens if tire pressure is too low?
faq.a: Low pressure causes edge wear, increased fuel consumption, and reduced handling performance.

faq.q: What happens if tire pressure is too high?
faq.a: High pressure causes center wear and reduces tire grip and comfort.

faq.q: What tire pressure should my spare tire have?
faq.a: Spare tires typically require 60 PSI or higher for safe long-term storage and use.

faq.q: Is this converter free to use?
